flags.message: This appears to be an antebellum mandatory-jurisdiction cause (Montgomery v. Hernandez, 12 Wheat. 129 (1827), a section-25 writ of error), not a cert petition, so the petition-disposition frame does not really apply. It seems to have stayed in predict scope because the snapshot and corpus row carry no dates or docket number for the era-based exclusion predicates to latch onto.

The snapshot is nearly bare: no docket entries, dates, docket number, or parties; the only substantive signals are the case name and one linked opinion cluster. The disposition looks recoverable from that cluster (a probe-recoverability candidate), which would let this event be resolved or excluded instead of predicted from a one-line record.
